summary: All critical Redis-outage events are present in evidence but role assignments are badly inverted: the early unrelated cart exception is labeled root_cause, the actual redis latency spike is demoted to context, and the causal chain ordering is obscured.
improvements: Correct role assignments: the redis latency spike at 12:13:00 is the trigger, ETIMEDOUT is the root_cause, and session/cart failures are consequences — not the pre-incident cart undefined-property error
Exclude or clearly flag the early cart exception cluster (11:50–11:52) as a pre-existing distractor rather than labeling it root_cause/consequence of the redis incident
